description: A `<style>` block fires E-STYLE-001 — scrml does not admit the element at all; CSS lives in `#{...}` (§4.17).
rationale: §4.17 (Raw-content elements), companion considerations: "`<script>` and `<style>` are NOT raw-content in scrml because scrml does not admit those elements at all — `<style>` triggers `E-STYLE-001` at the block-splitter level (CSS lives in `#{...}`)". The rejection is unconditional on the ELEMENT, not on its body: `.btn { color: red; }` is well-formed CSS, and it is the `<style>` element itself that is not scrml. Presence only — the message text, the auto-convert hint, and the skip-to-`</style>` recovery are impl freedom. Paired with style-001-scoped-css-neg, which expresses this exact styling intent in the idiomatic `#{}` form and stays silent.
